question: In SAP Ariba Product Sourcing, why must the item status transition sequentially through “Ready → Processing → Success” — rather than directly from “Ready” to “Success” — when the system copies contract pricing from the Placeholder Plant to the Actual Plants and creates Purchase Information Records (PIRs)? What critical validation and processing logic executed in the Ariba backend does this status transition mechanism reflect?
ground_truth: This status transition (Ready → Processing → Success) is not merely a superficial UI display change; rather, it strictly corresponds to the critical lifecycle phases of the backend asynchronous batch processing, reflecting Ariba’s enforced logic for data consistency, transactional integrity, and business compliance. The specific reasoning is as follows:
context:
10.The status of the items changes from Ready to Processing, and then to Success when PIRs are created.
Related Information
Copying the Contracted Price from the Placeholder Plant to Actual Plants and Create PIRs [page 186]
SAP Ariba Product Sourcing Guide
Bill of Materials and Materials Management PUBLIC 173

question: Through which two primary methods does SAP Ariba allow users to modify public master data?
ground_truth: Use Workspaces and Tasks (in Ariba Administration) and edit the CSV file, then run the Data Import task.
context:
Common Master Data for SAP Ariba Strategic
Sourcing Solutions Management
Common master data refers to information used by all SAP Ariba strategic sourcing solutions, such as users,
groups, suppliers, and commodity codes. After the initial set of common master data is loaded for you by SAP
Ariba, you can modify (update, delete and add) the common master data yourself.
You can modify common master data using workspaces and tasks in the Ariba Administrator You can also modify
common master data by editing comma-separated values (CSV) files and running data import tasks from the Ariba
Administrator.
We recommend using a data management system to track all your data changes. Before deleting data, you should
export the data to CSV files by running data export tasks. You can then use these CSV files to reimport your data, if
necessary. Regularly exporting data can also help you keep track of your current configuration.
Manage Data Using Workspaces and Tasks in the Administration Area
The administration area includes workspaces and tasks that let you maintain some of your data. You typically use

question: What is the value of the Status element in the DocumentStatusChangeNotification request sent by SAP Ariba when an event is closed?
context:
multiple participants or multiple items).
• The event manager checks the banner (top) area of the contents page to view the status of the Retrieve
document URLs operation. The event manager also checks the status (Info field) displayed for each
document URL in the event Content or Summary area.
• The event manager publishes the event. SAP Ariba sends a DocumentURLOutBound request. The request
includes a Status element with the value RFXDocument:Publish.
If an error occurs when sending the DocumentURLOutBound request or processing the response, SAP Ariba
displays a warning but allows the event to be published.
Participants viewing event contents can click the Document URL to view documents hosted on the buyer's
server.
• The preview time for the event elapses and the event is open for participants to send responses.
SAP Ariba sends a DocumentStatusChangeNotification request that includes a Status element with the value
RFXDocument:MoveToOpen.
• When the event closes, SAP Ariba sends a DocumentStatusChangeNotification request that includes a Status
element with the value RFXDocument:close. The buyer can choose to disable the document URLs.

question: SAP Ariba recommends the maximum number of items that can be added to a single questionnaire to avoid gradual performance degradation.

approval task to restart the approval flow.
• As with the engagement request and engagement-level risk assessments, you can enable ad hoc approval. You
can also enable ad hoc review. With issue management projects, if you specify an empty approval or review
flow for the task, the project owner (rather than a member of the Supplier Risk Engagement Governance
Analyst group) can add approvers or reviewers. An approval or review flow is empty when there are no nodes

• The base language of the issue management project template's default survey document is English, and
therefore default content in that survey document is English. You can add translations [page 382] to that
default content for users in non-English locales.
• SAP Ariba recommends that you add no more than 500 pieces of content to an individual questionnaire.
Adding more than 500 pieces of content can cause performance to degrade progressively as you add more
content.

question: Does the Ariba documentation mention whether a questionnaire can be configured with an expiration date? If so, what related information is included?
ground_truth: Yes, surveys can be configured with an expiration date; the relevant information includes “whether the survey can expire and, if so, its expiration schedule.”
context:
• Whether the questionnaire is internal or external and, for internal questionnaires, its recipients.
• The content of the questionnaire.
• Whether or not the questionnaire can expire and, if so, its expiration schedule.
• Whether or not the questionnaire has its own approval flow and if so, who is responsible for approving it.
• Whether or not updates to the questionnaire also require approvals.

question: What user group or team member role is required to create or edit a template?
ground_truth: You must be a member of the global Template Creator group or a member of the Templates Creator team for the template project.
context:

create or edit templates.

question: Why does the Carbon Footprint Questionnaire in SAP Ariba Sourcing use dynamic answer types instead of traditional fixed options, and how does this design simultaneously support compliance with the PACT v2 standard and the business objective of sustainable development footprint management?
ground_truth: The Carbon Footprint Questionnaire employs dynamic answer types (e.g., configurable numeric inputs, hierarchical industry classification selections, multidimensional activity data entry) to precisely align with the core Scope 3 emissions data collection requirements defined in the World Business Council for Sustainable Development (WBCSD) PACT v2 framework. PACT v2 emphasizes “activity-based data” and “value chain context awareness”—that is, differentiated baseline activity data (e.g., ton-kilometers transported, kWh energy consumed, kg raw materials used) must be collected across industries and value chain stages (e.g., raw material procurement, logistics, outsourced manufacturing), then combined with corresponding emission factors to calculate carbon emissions. Traditional static-option questionnaires cannot accommodate heterogeneous data requirements spanning industries and processes, leading easily to information distortion or forced categorization bias—violating PACT v2’s principle of “data granularity matching actual operations.” In contrast, dynamic answer types enable buyers to flexibly configure question logic by category or supplier tier within Ariba Sourcing (e.g., enabling a “transport mode + distance + payload” triplet input for logistics service providers; enabling structured fields for “energy type consumed in production + annual usage volume + process stage” for Tier-1 manufacturers). This ensures collected data complies with PACT v2’s definition of activity data as “verifiable, traceable, and contextually complete,” while also enabling seamless parsing and mapping by SAP Sustainability Footprint Management into its embedded GHG Protocol classification model and regionalized emission factor library—thereby supporting high-integrity Scope 3 accounting, benchmarking, and decarbonization pathway analysis. This capability constitutes the critical prerequisite for scientific decision-making and compliant disclosure in sustainability footprint management.
context:
SAP Ariba Sourcing Integration with SAP
Sustainability Footprint Management
The integration between SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Sustainability Footprint Management enables you to collect
carbon footprint data from supplier responses in SAP Ariba Sourcing and send that data to SAP Sustainability
Footprint Management.
Carbon footprint data is collected from suppliers using the Carbon Footprint Questionnaire. When suppliers
are awarded, their responses to the Carbon Footprint Questionnaire are sent from SAP Ariba Sourcing to SAP
Sustainability Footprint Management.
World Business Council for Sustainable Development (WBCSD), has established the Pathfinder Framework (PACT)
as a global standard of calculating and exchanging scope 3 emission data across the value chain. The Carbon
Footprint Questionnaire is designed to provide a more versatile and robust way of gathering data in the PACT V.2
standard and complies with sustainability footprint management. Unlike traditional fixed-choice answers, dynamic
answer types allow for a wider range of responses. Suppliers can choose from predefined, industry-standard

question: In an Ariba Guided Sourcing event, how should event rules be configured if the buyer wishes to strictly restrict suppliers to adding only supplier-added items and lots exclusively within Alternative Bids—and prohibit them from adding any non-predefined items or lots in the Primary Bid? This configuration must comply with system logic constraints; additionally, the synergistic effect of the relevant rules and the corresponding documentation references must be specified.
ground_truth: To achieve this business objective, **two event rules must be enabled simultaneously**:
context:
Allowing Suppliers to Add Items and Lots to Alternative
Bids
To collect content that suppliers might want to provide in addition to those defined in a guided sourcing event, you
can enable the Allow suppliers to add items event rule to allow suppliers to add items and lots to both primary and
alternative bids.
If you only want suppliers to add items and lots to alternative bids, you can enable both Allow suppliers to add
items and Allow supplier-added items only in alternative bids event rules.
For more information about supplier-added items, refer to Supplier-Added Items in Guided Sourcing Events [page
486].
Parent topic: Alternative Bidding in Guided Sourcing Events [page 448]
Related Information
Alternative Bidding Rules for Guided Sourcing Events [page 449]
Monitoring Alternative Bids in Guided Sourcing [page 451]
Including Alternative Bids in Bid Ranking for Guided Sourcing Events [page 452]
Supporting Multi-Currency for Alternative Bids in Guided Sourcing [page 453]
Awarding Alternative Bids in Guided Sourcing [page 454]
Hiding Alternative Bids in Guided Sourcing [page 455]
Creating Guided Sourcing Events with Alternative Bidding [page 456]

question: What is the maximum number of characters allowed in the Title field of the Ariba Contract Workspace?
context:
Title
The Title field specifies the title of the contract workspace.
The Title can contain up to 254 characters and cannot contain any of the following special characters:
\ / : ? “ < > | # + % &
 Note
In addition, do not end the title with a period (.).
ContractId
The ContractId specifies a contract identification number. This number is different from the InternalID
generated for each contract workspace by SAP Ariba Contracts, and can be any string you choose provided it
is unique. The ContractId is used when a company specific naming or identification scheme is needed.
If you specified a value other than ContractId as the WorkspaceLookupKey in the
ImportProjectParameters.csv file, you can leave this field blank and the system generates a contract ID
for you. However, if you are using ContractId as the WorkspaceLookupKey, you must specify a value in this field.
 Note
Each contract must have a unique ContractId. Different versions of the same contract can either share a
ContractId, or can all be unique. SAP Ariba recommends that you assign a unique ContractId to each

question: What is the maximum hierarchical level supported by templates in SAP Ariba?
context:
• A form consists of layouts, and layouts contain questions (multiple choice questions) or input (text) fields
depending on whether you're creating a questionnaire or a scorecard.
• You can place additional layouts within a layout. Placing layouts within an existing layout is called nesting.
Nesting is useful in creating a hierarchy for your template. Hierarchy is a grouping or classification of questions
or text fields in your template based on their similarity. For example, you can have a main layout on a topic
called "Quality". You can then nest related sub-layouts related to quality (say product quality and service
quality) under this main layout. Such a hierarchical design lends structure and readability to your template. A
hierarchy level of 4 is supported for creating templates. This means that you can nest layouts up to 3 levels
only, and the last level must be an input text field or a multiple choice question.
• You drag a widget from the widget panel into a layout.
• Drag another layout into the form. You can either choose to nest the layout in the existing layout or place it
separately above or below the existing layout.

About Exporting and Importing the Clause Library
If you are the Clause Library Project Owner, or if you are on the Clause Library team and a member of the Contract
Administrator group, you can export the Clause Library to a ZIP file. Clause Library export and import allows you to
use a Clause Library that has been developed in another environment without having to manually re-create it. For
example, you can develop your Clause Library in a test environment, export it, and then import it into a production

question: In the SAP Ariba Risk Management module, if a customer wishes to quantitatively assess the overall risk posture at the supplier level based on risk domains and support continuous monitoring of its trend over time, which two configuration capabilities should be enabled? Please explain the respective functions of each capability and their synergistic relationship, referencing the documentation.
ground_truth: The following two features must be enabled:
context:
Allow no-effectiveness option for control review. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .472
Allowable number of suppliers for bulk creation of engagement requests. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .473

Allow user permissions to specific administrator actions. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .475
Allow using control effectiveness levels to evaluate residual risk by risk domain. . . . . . . . . . . . . 475
Allow using issues to evaluate residual risk by risk domain. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .476
Automatically skip sending assessments . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .477
Calculate engagement level residual risk by risk domain. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .477
Calculate inherent risk for engagements by risk domain . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 479
Calculate supplier level inherent and residual risk by risk domain. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 480
